summary: Extraction-fidelity review accepts the candidate. The coverage assessment, research session, search log, and screening run consistently describe a blocked no-op review-landscape pass: no PubMed hit list was retrieved, no source snapshots were created, no screening decisions were made, and no systematic-review or meta-analysis source was added. The proposed records preserve that limitation instead of converting it into an evidence claim.

findings.claim_or_issue: The target candidate claims only a blocked no-op search and screening trail for the senolytics review-landscape gap.
findings.why_it_matters: The research session, search log, screening run, and coverage assessment all carry the same blocker: no source row or context pack supplied a candidate review source and PubMed/NLM retrieval was unavailable, so no extracted review evidence should appear.
findings.recommended_action: No record edit is needed for this lane; preserve the blocker until a network-enabled, source-snapshot-backed screening pass is run.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed records do not add a systematic-review or meta-analysis source, source snapshot, text snapshot, eligibility decision, or review-derived finding.
findings.why_it_matters: Extraction fidelity would fail if the candidate upgraded the review landscape using an unavailable PubMed result or unsnapshotted source, but the candidate leaves hits, decisions, and included source IDs empty.
findings.recommended_action: Accept this lane and require any future review-landscape upgrade to cite concrete retained PubMed or full-text source snapshots.

findings.claim_or_issue: The coverage assessment continues to mark recent_reviews_meta_analyses as thin and keeps the senolytics-review-landscape known gap open.
findings.why_it_matters: The no-op repair remains faithful only if the unresolved systematic-review/meta-analysis gap is visible to future triage and synthesis work.
findings.recommended_action: No change is needed for extraction fidelity; future work should close the gap only after durable search, screening, and source-snapshot records exist.
